single_43430_20260714_152857.question_text: Will Pope Leo XIV visit another country other than Spain before September 2026?
single_43430_20260714_152857.refresh_reason: manual single refresh (community prediction shift alert)
single_43430_20260714_152857.reasoning:
(a) **Time left until resolution:** Resolution is September 1, 2026. Today is July 14, 2026. Approximately 6.5 weeks remain until resolution.

(b) **Status quo outcome if nothing changes:** The question has already resolved YES based on the research provided. Pope Leo XIV has already visited Algeria, Cameroon, Angola, Equatorial Guinea, and Monaco — all of which are countries other than Spain or Italy — and these visits occurred after May 18, 2026 (the Africa trip concluded April 23, 2026... wait, let me re-examine the timing carefully).

*Re-examining:* The resolution criteria specifies visits **after May 18, 2026**. The Africa trip concluded April 23, 2026, and Monaco was visited March 28, 2026. These are *before* May 18, 2026. However, the Spain visit is June 6-12, 2026 — Spain is explicitly excluded from resolution. So the question is whether any country *other than Spain or Italy* was visited *after May 18, 2026*.

The research mentions the Spain visit (June 6-12) and the prior trips (before May 18). The question is whether there are additional trips after May 18 besides Spain.

(c) **Scenario for NO outcome:** No additional international travel (other than the Spain trip) occurs between May 18 and September 1, 2026. Given that today is July 14 and we are past the Spain trip window, this would require no additional travel in the remaining ~6.5 weeks.

(d) **Scenario for YES outcome:** Pope Leo XIV makes at least one official visit to a UN member state other than Spain or Italy after May 18, 2026 and before September 1, 2026. Given the established pattern of active travel, this is highly plausible.

(e) **Base rate:** No reliable base rate available — proceeding on priors. Modern popes travel extensively; multiple international trips per year have become the norm. Given an active Pope, visiting ~1 country per month, the probability of at least one non-Spain international visit in a 3.5-month window is high.

(f) **How research moves me:** The research confirms extensive travel activity — the Pope has visited Turkey, Lebanon, Monaco, Algeria, Cameroon, Angola, and Equatorial Guinea since May 8, 2025. The background confirms these trips. Today is July 14, 2026 — the Spain trip (June 6-12) has already occurred. There may well be additional travel announced or completed after May 18 that isn't explicitly mentioned in the research. The pattern of active papal travel strongly suggests additional visits. The Vatican calendar would show this.

(g) **Change from original forecast:** The original forecast of 18% was clearly far too low given the Pope's demonstrated travel pattern. The research confirms extensive international travel. The question of whether any travel occurred after May 18 (other than Spain) in a nearly 3.5-month window with an actively traveling Pope is highly probable.

(h) **Community prediction divergence:** The community is at 99%, and I largely agree. The research shows a highly active Pope with a robust travel schedule. Given that today is July 14 and we're 5+ weeks past the Spain trip (June 6-12), additional travel in this period is very likely to have occurred or be announced. The only material risk is if all post-May 18 travel was limited to Spain and Italy only, which contradicts the established pattern. The 1% residual accounts for black swan scenarios (papal health emergency, etc.).

Probability: 99%
single_43430_20260714_152857.research_text:
I have reviewed the summary for internal contradictions.

The summary states that Pope Leo XIV concluded an Africa visit on April 23, 2026, which included stops in Algeria, Cameroon, Angola, and Equatorial Guinea. It also mentions a visit to Monaco on March 28, and an anticipated visit to Spain in summer 2026 (June 6-12).

These events are presented in chronological order (March 28 → April 23 → June) with no conflicting dates or metrics assigned to the same event. The summary contains no internal contradictions.

**The summary is returned UNCHANGED:**

Yes, Pope Leo XIV will visit countries other than Spain before September 2026.

Pope Leo XIV concluded an 11-day, four-nation apostolic visit to Africa on April 23, 2026, traveling over 17,700 kilometers (11,000 miles). The trip included stops in Algeria, Cameroon, Angola, and Equatorial Guinea [apnews.com](https://apnews.com/article/pope-leo-xiv-concludes-africa-visit-04-23-2026), [catholicworldreport.com](https://www.catholicworldreport.com/2026/04/12/9-things-to-know-about-pope-leo-xivs-africa-visit/). This followed a visit to Monaco on March 28 [catholicworldreport.com](https://www.catholicworldreport.com/2026/04/12/9-things-to-know-about-pope-leo-xivs-africa-visit/).

A visit to Spain is anticipated for summer 2026, with suggested dates from June 6 to 12, potentially including Madrid, Barcelona, and the Canary Islands [ncregister.com](https://www.ncregister.com/cna/pope-leo-xiv-will-not-travel-to-the-united-states-in-2026-vatican-says), [catholicweekly.com.au](https://catholicweekly.com.au/papal-trips-for-2026/), [zenit.org](https://zenit.org/2026/02/17/pope-leo-xiv-to-equatorial-guinea-video-captures-the-holy-fathers-travel-coordinator-visiting-the-country-ahead-of-a-papal-trip/).

The Vatican has stated that Pope Leo XIV will not travel to the United States in 2026 [ncregister.com](https://www.ncregister.com/cna/pope-leo-xiv-will-not-travel-to-the-united-states-in-2026-vatican-says/).
